review_title: Perfect IDE for writting simple and complex codes
description: It helps us to build simple and complex codes without any problems and freely.
author: Houyamne A.
pros: I prefer Wing Python over many others because it supports both simple and complex codes in nature. It's a target-driven IDE which means "it's goal-oriented and performs only specific tasks".I don't trust multi or can-do-all-by-themselves coding software as they are never perfect in anything.Wing Python IDE enables us to write Python minified codes without hassles. It's code-centric and serves its purpose perfectly.We do not really use it for debugging, however, we use it to conduct simple verification across our projects and sometimes for testing (where necessary).It automatically indents my lines of code and shows proof of its smart and collaborative nature and it's also beginner friendly.
cons: To my knowledge, There is no feature that needs improvement for now.

review_title: Wing Python IDE: A Much Intelligent IDE than Existing Solutions
description: Great experience that makes developing in Python with its plethora of libraries very rapid and integrated. I would definitely prefer it over PyCharm in many different development domains even in the field on socket and client/server programming.
author: Maha S.
pros: I have switched to Wing Python IDE after using PyCharm for several years. It seems that this IDE is so well designed to make the development much easier with code autocompletion and a very elegant debugger. Python has a lot of libraries in many different domains. I used it for developing home automation software, scientific computing, AI and ML model development and training, in addition to designing client/server solution. What makes Wing great is that once you import the needed libraries, code auto completion becomes so graceful and make the development very efficient and rapid. It is very fancy in terms of hierarchically presenting the code layers which makes debugging and even team work much more productive. One of the best IDEs for Python with a trial version and a cost effective pro version.
cons: Definitely would have been happier to have a free version for the pro features. But this is totally justified considering the amazing code development features available in Wing. Some crashes and hangs are experienced from time to time but not a big deal considering the intelligent code editor, debugger, and unit testing features

author: Abdelrahman E.
pros: It displays errors in a very efficient manner with an autocomplete feature which saves a lot of developer's time and effort. the company is focused to keep this app updated with new features and handling any old issues.
cons: I didn't face so much problems using this ide exept the dark theme. and it's very expensive to renew the subscribtion after the end of the first trial.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I like WingPython IDE due to its powerful error debugger and easy navigation through code.
author: Maaz M.
pros: Wing Python IDE is a lightweight, easy to use development software hosting powerful code editor, along with code testing capability and built-in error debugging. Its customized keystrokes shortcuts along with with its highly flexible macros further increases its accessibility.
cons: Thoghu Wing Python IDE is a powerful developer yet it offers a very minimal support to languages that aren't python based which makes prevents it from wide use.

review_title: Very convenient tool to develop Python program
description: All things considered I think that although it's expensive compared to other IDE for Python, Wing IDE is worth it. It has so many cool features, including very useful integrated debugger.
author: Artyom A.
pros: Wing Python IDE has very useful features as built-in debugger, checks for errors in the source code, super-flexible macro capabilities.
cons: Interface is a little bit old fashioned. But I prefer to have the features to beauty. It's also expensive for small enterprises.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great all-around Python IDE and debugger
description: I've been using Wing IDE as my main development environment for 10 years now. I'm a big fan of Wing. I've used it for my open-source projects, my client projects when I was working as a freelancer, and now I use it at my work in a corporate environment. I do Python programming almost exclusively, so its Python-centric approach is a good fit for me. The debugger is first-class. It works on multi-process, multi-thread programs. You have your breakpoints, watches, step commands and everything you can expect from a modern debugger. Remote debugging is supported, which is especially crucial in a corporate environment, where you're debugging code that runs on different machines. They've recently added advanced features like recursive debugging, which helped me do some deep troubleshooting at my job. The editor is great. It's got VI and eMacs mode and it's extensible with scripts. It has lots of useful tools (like showing documentation, refactoring, diffing, Python shell, etc.) which I spread across 2 big monitors. The support staff is great. I've made many suggestions and requests for improvement to them over the years and they've implemented many of them. Bugs are fixed quickly. Overall, I highly recommend Wing IDE!
author: Ram R.
pros: - First-class debugger - Great, extensible editor - Cross-compatible with Windows / Linux / Mac - Responsive email support - Extensive set of tools
cons: - I can't get it to fill out my taxes :)

description: I work with wingide (pro) since 5 years. I did try to change it with some other ide totally free... but no one gives to me same level of productivity.
author: Alessandro C.
pros: Debugger. I work with zope/plone enviroments and the ability do place simple brackpoints directly on a code line without import any pdb and without restart the process is, IMHO, what's worth of it. It is written in python so is really efficient in running python code against other editors written in java o c#
cons: Editor is a little bit old. Others IDE add graphic facilities that make them really cool. WingIDE lack of coolness but. Some recent meta languages haven't their syntaxt hilight (for eample. react jsx)

review_title: My Indispensable Tool for Python Development (every day)
description: Using Wing IDE has increased my productivity. The debugger especially has reduced time spent fixing bugs. Writing Python code has become more FUN.
author: Douglas W. G.
pros: From the beginning I found the product had wonderful usability. Wing has tons of features and it takes time to learn to take advantage of them. From day 1, I found my development more comfortable. I developed a large web application based on python CGI scripts. I found certain features, like the remote debugging, to be a thrill. After using Wing for a very short time, I upgraded to the Pro version, which I have now been using almost a decade. The support from Wingware is outstanding. They really care about what the users are experiencing and they address problems promptly. Their IDE product is about two decades old. That takes a lot of commitment. The program is always scurrying around providing valuable information. Right clicking or hovering provides fast access to valuable features.
cons: That's a tough one. I am at a loss to think of anything, honestly.

review_title: A Python IDE that release the power of your mind
description: We are a company providing Natural Langugae Processing solutions, especially in Mandarin. The Stack Data and Debugging tools that can show Chinese characters correctly at some breakpoint saves us a lot of time. We really have a good time at work with such a great tool.
author: Wen-jet W.
pros: It's intellectual editor response and fast launch speed help me focus on the logic behind my code. Not to mention the prompt and friendly responses from Support/Sales and mailing list makes us feel like we are astronuts working in the deep space, and always having a solid support team making sure our gears are right for the mission.
cons: I get lost in the full-functioned preference settings often.

description: I have used Wing for so many years I can't even remember when I started. It is a joy to use. The features just keep coming year after year. Customer support is ridiculously responsive and on target, with responses immediately disseminated to all users. The Wing guys have extraordinarily deep understanding of Python, frameworks, platforms, debugging, and user needs, and a great imagination for features and capabilities.
author: Mitchell M.
pros: huge feature set, integrated support for many frameworks, external & network debugging, tremendous cross-platform support and choice of keyboard "personality", wonderfully customizable, highly flexible arrangement of windows/tabs/tools, powerful editing and debugging facilities
cons: There is nothing whatsoever that I dislike about this software.

review_title: Ligera, rápida que nos ahorra dinero en el servidory sus peticiones
description: Una herramienta increíblemente ligera y potente a la vez. Empezamos con la versión gratuita, pero creemos que vamos a pasarnos a la versión de pago y seguir aprendiendo con ella. Aun estamos verdad en su implementación, pero sobre todo el control de versiones en Git nos hace que nos decidamos por ella
author: Rafael A.
pros: Lo mejor de todo es lo ligero que es. Nos permite ahorra dinero mensualmente en el ahorro de servidor/peticiones
cons: Que acostumbrado a otras interfaz hace que este un poco obsoleta, pero lo cubre su rapidez, así que no es determinante para nosotros
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: Ho utilizzato Wing come ammbiente di sviluppo per piccole automazioni nella mia routine. Wing mi ha permesso di svilupparle in un tempo ragionevole, aumentando così la mia efficienza.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Le caratteristiche migliori di Wing sono le interfacce user-friendly e la funzionalità di debugging.
cons: La dashboard principale è molto basilare, potrebbe spiazzare a primo impatto chi è abituato a sviluppare avendo sempre sotto controllo tutto l'insieme del progetto.

review_title: Light weight full featured Python IDE for developers
description: It creates a superb development envinronment for Python coders. I used it for software and application development purposes with lots of options and flexibility of customization.
author: Anik T.
pros: Wing Python (WP) IDE is the most light weight software for python programming i ever used and I am impressed with the user interface and power code editing options, simple debugging capacities with testing.
cons: It is literally overprice to use as IDE as there are some more affordable and low price or open source options in the market.

description: Overall it's a great powerful tool for Python programming which provides a full range of features.
author: Awais A.
pros: Provided simple, clean, and minimalist interface which makes writing code easier and helps to focus on the main task which is writing code without a lot of distracting features.
cons: Adding new packages can be tricky and the price of a pro license is on the higher side. It also doesn't support any other programming language.

description: I began to use Wing once I started dealing with more complex projects. Wing is great for managing large sets of files and getting results instantly.
author: Nicholas N.
pros: The IDE allows me to write my code and execute it all within the same window. Far easier to work with than IDLE.
cons: The UI design seems outdated (mostly black and white). This could lead to difficulty using the IDE if you have eyestrain.

description: I use Wing both for my research projects and for teaching Python for beginners. I think Wing is able to be customized for both kind of tasks
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Wing is a very easy to use IDE for Python. I use it for teaching since it provides an intuitive user interface which do not overwhelm an user who's learning to program. Wing combines with Python philosophy of making things neat and clear
cons: One thing that I missed in previous versions of Wing was the small set of palettes for coloring syntax.

description: for a professional programmer, wing python IDE is a good software.
author: Ajumon R.
pros: For professional python programming, Wing Python IDE Software is good. it is expensive, but it provides many tools for programming. i like the intelligent editing tool. using this tool, we can write program without errors.
cons: its expensive software. after 1 month of trial use, we have to buy this software. if they provide a free lite version, we can use this software for personal use also.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: 1. Justifies the logo as being lightweight and operating faster compared to other IDEs.2. Variety of features involved.3. User-friendly interface and easy to use.
cons: 1. Unlike other IDEs, only supports Python language.2. Comparatively expensive.

description: I got phyton lesson in college and did hobby projects
author: Bugra S.
pros: best python ide for fast programming. wing101 is enough for simple and not very professional codes
cons: not very efficient if you are going to do heavy code with a large dataset and a long time.

author: Bruno Nicholas S.
pros: Best python error display format I've experienced which makes debugging that much easier
cons: Trial period is for only 30 days. It takes sometime to get familiar with the interface and tools

description: A very good program.
author: Ella G.
pros: I like how intuitive the software is for beginners.
cons: The software often crashes as it is supposed to run, which makes it hard to use for bigger projects.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: I'm a fan of Wingide and I'm happy to admit it.
description: I have been using Wingide pro for a long time - I believe I started with version 1 or 2 and for the past 10 years have been using it every day for python development at my work supporting a GUI application that allows calibration and diagnostics of a distance measurement system. The current version of the application has been in development for 5 years, uses a range of classic python packages including numpy matplotlib and wxpython and has grown to include a large number of python modules. However, Wingide has been able to handle everything I have been able to through at it with out any problems. I also use Visual Studio of a fairly regular basis fro c++ development and think that Wingide is every bit as good and in someways better than Visual Studio . If you are in the market for a professional level python IDE you really owe it to yourself to, at least, try Wingide.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Things I really appreciate about wingide (pro): The publisher 's have a civilized pricing and licensing approach. The developers behind the product are very attentive to user feedback - they respond quickly and with clear reasonable answers concerning problems. When I have reported issues I have typically got a decent response on the same day. Unlike the case for some IDEs (I'm looking at you eclipse) creating and managing a project in Wingide is not a pain. Easy to debug into running python projects. The debugging ability in wingide is excellent . The debug console tab is very handy for example - hit a break point change the current context and then continue. Happiness. Easy navigation around a project with things like the go to definition feature or find points of use feature Runs on multiple operating systems - I did not have to learn a new tools when I moved over to developing on Linux. Good documentation.
cons: Honestly nothing really has upset me about this software - I normally try to use free, opensource products but I am happy to pay for Wingide and have encouraged a number of my colleagues to start using it and they seem happy with the product too. I'm aware that there are now a number of other decent python ides out there (thank you people - it was not so good in 1998 -1999 when I started working with python) but Wingide just works so nicely for me that I have not felt any desire "to play the field" looking at the competition.

review_title: My Python IDE of choice after trying several
description: I have been using Wing IDE for over 9 year and it has never failed to allow me to allow me detect and correct any problem with the code. The ability to auto-setup projects, environments, attach to Anaconda environments, notebooks etc is first rate. As for support there is none better in the software world that I have experienced. Literally minutes after I post a problem (during normal working hours - it might take a couple hours otherwise) there is a response with a suggestion, solution or acknowledgement of an issue. It is really unbelievable support.
author: Jeff V.
pros: I have the professional version which provides the Debug Probe tool. This tool is excellent as it allows you to inspect and change variable values, execute code etc at the stack level that you are at or move to. This is far more sophisticated that most other IDE's ability to change a variable value. The ability to "attach" the debugger to executing Python code that was started outside the debugger is another great feature when you are trying to see the code execute in its actual environment.
cons: Until recently I did not like the fact that is did not alert the user to unknown variables, as the tool has excellent auto-completion. Wing 7 in now in pre-release and I am using it does this and several other things. This feature is very useful. Based on your code type an looping the debugger can cause a slow down. I'm not sure this is a problem isolated to Wing as I have seen the same thing when using PyCharm.

description: Overall experience has been good and it is working fine. And i am looking forward to work it to better advantage..
author: Nishant K.
pros: Most likable feature is the platform that helps the developers to write and deploy code to maximum benefits and accountability.
cons: Least likable feature is the customer support but at the same time if the interface is working fine then there is no need of it..It needs a little more of the third party collaboration and overall it is good
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Durante i miei studi universitari mi è capitato di utilizzare Wing Phyton IDE per un mio progetto e, alla fin dei conti, la mia esperienza è stata positiva.Essendo esperiente in materia, ho visto l'enorme potenzialità che questo prodotto ha da offrire ma riconosco che per i meno esperti possa risultare caotico. Risulta un piacere programmare in quanto il debugger è sempre preciso e ti aiuta molto a capire dove stai sbagliando. Inoltre con l'autocompletamento puoi anche risparmiare tempo nel scrivere codice. Promosso.
author: Gioacchino T.
pros: Interfaccia utente semplice e intuitiva che rende semplice la navigazione tra i file e le funzionalità dell'IDE. Strumento di debug abbastanza potente e molto preciso per individuare errori nel codice.
cons: Sul mio computer risulta molto pesante. Per i meno esperti può risultare confusionario all'inizio.
